randomNumber:
-module(random_demo).
-export([run/0]).

run() ->
    % Generate a random float between 0.0 and 1.0
    RandomFloat = rand:uniform(),
    io:format("Random Float [0.0-1.0): ~f~n", [RandomFloat]),

    % Generate a random integer from 1 to 100 (inclusive)
    Min = 1,
    Max = 100,
    % rand:uniform(N) gives 1..N
    RandomInt = rand:uniform(Max - Min + 1) + Min - 1,
    io:format("Random Integer [~p-~p]: ~p~n", [Min, Max, RandomInt]),

    % Select a random element from a list
    Fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"],
    RandomFruit = lists:nth(rand:uniform(length(Fruits)), Fruits),
    io:format("Random fruit: ~s~n", [RandomFruit]).
reverseString:
-module(reverse_demo).
-export([run/0]).

run() ->
    Original = "Hello, Erlang!",
    % lists:reverse/1 works on strings (which are lists of chars)
    Reversed = lists:reverse(Original),

    io:format("Original: ~s~n", [Original]),
    io:format("Reversed: ~s~n", [Reversed]),

    % Works correctly with Unicode code points
    OriginalUnicode = "你好 Erlang",
    io:format("Original Unicode: ~ts~n", [OriginalUnicode]),
    io:format("Reversed Unicode: ~ts~n", [lists:reverse(OriginalUnicode)]).
fibonacci:
-module(fib_demo).
-export([fib/1, run/0]).

% Public function
fib(N) when is_integer(N), N >= 0 ->
    fib(N, 0, 1).

% Tail-recursive helper function
fib(0, A, _) -> A;
fib(N, A, B) when N > 0 ->
    fib(N - 1, B, A + B).

run() ->
    NumTerms = 15,
    Sequence = [fib(I) || I <- lists:seq(0, NumTerms - 1)],
    io:format("First ~p Fibonacci numbers:~n~p~n", [NumTerms, Sequence]),

    % Erlang has arbitrary-precision integers, so large numbers are fine
    io:format("F(90) = ~p~n", [fib(90)]).
factorial:
-module(factorial_demo).
-export([factorial/1, run/0]).

% Public function
factorial(N) when is_integer(N), N >= 0 ->
    factorial(N, 1).

% Tail-recursive helper function with an accumulator
factorial(0, Acc) -> Acc;
factorial(N, Acc) when N > 0 ->
    factorial(N - 1, N * Acc).

run() ->
    Num = 5,
    io:format("Factorial of ~p is ~p~n", [Num, factorial(Num)]),

    NumZero = 0,
    io:format("Factorial of ~p is ~p~n", [NumZero, factorial(NumZero)]),

    % Erlang handles BigInts automatically
    NumLarge = 30,
    io:format("Factorial of ~p is ~p~n", [NumLarge, factorial(NumLarge)]).
primeCheck:
-module(prime_demo).
-export([is_prime/1, run/0]).

% Main function with initial checks
is_prime(N) when not is_integer(N) -> false;
is_prime(N) when N =< 1 -> false;
is_prime(N) when N =< 3 -> true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 =:= 0 orelse N rem 3 =:= 0 -> false;
is_prime(N) ->
    Limit = trunc(math:sqrt(N)),
    has_divisor(N, 5, Limit).

% Helper to check for divisors from 5 upwards
has_divisor(_, I, Limit) when I > Limit ->
    true; % No divisors found
has_divisor(N, I, Limit) when N rem I =:= 0 orelse N rem (I + 2) =:= 0 ->
    false; % Divisor found
has_divisor(N, I, Limit) ->
    has_divisor(N, I + 6, Limit).

run() ->
    NumbersToCheck = [2, 3, 4, 17, 25, 29, 97, 104729],
    lists:foreach(fun(N) ->
        case is_prime(N) of
            true -> io:format("~p is prime.~n", [N]);
            false -> io:format("~p is not prime.~n", [N])
        end
    end, NumbersToCheck).
readFile:
-module(readfile_demo).
-export([run/0]).

run() ->
    Filename = "erlang_read_test.txt",

    % Create a dummy file for reading
    Content = "First line from Erlang.\nSecond line.\n",
    ok = file:write_file(Filename, Content),
    io:format("Created dummy file: ~s~n~n", [Filename]),

    io:format("--- Reading file '~s' ---~n", [Filename]),
    case file:read_file(Filename) of
        {ok, BinaryContent} ->
            io:format("File content as binary:~n~p~n", [BinaryContent]),
            io:format("File content as string:~n~s~n", [binary_to_list(BinaryContent)]);
        {error, Reason} ->
            io:format("Error reading file: ~p~n", [Reason])
    end,
    
    % Clean up
    file:delete(Filename).
writeFile:
-module(writefile_demo).
-export([run/0]).

run() ->
    Filename = "output_erlang.txt",

    % 1. Overwrite or create a new file
    io:format("--- Writing (Overwrite) to '~s' ---~n", [Filename]),
    Content1 = "This line overwrites existing content.\n",
    case file:write_file(Filename, Content1) of
        ok -> io:format("Content written successfully.~n");
        {error, Reason} -> io:format("Error writing file: ~p~n", [Reason])
    end,

    % 2. Append to the existing file
    io:format("~n--- Appending to '~s' ---~n", [Filename]),
    Content2 = "This line was appended.\n",
    case file:write_file(Filename, Content2, [append]) of
        ok -> io:format("Content appended successfully.~n");
        {error, Reason} -> io:format("Error appending to file: ~p~n", [Reason])
    end.
fileExists:
-module(fileexists_demo).
-export([run/0]).

check_path(Path) ->
    io:format("~nChecking '~s':~n", [Path]),
    io:format("  -> Is regular file? ~p~n", [filelib:is_regular(Path)]),
    io:format("  -> Is directory? ~p~n", [filelib:is_dir(Path)]).

run() ->
    FileToCheck = "erlang.json", % Should exist
    DirToCheck = ".",             % Current directory
    NonExistent = "no_such_file.erl",

    io:format("--- Checking for files and directories ---~n"),
    check_path(FileToCheck),
    check_path(DirToCheck),
    check_path(NonExistent).
dateTime:
-module(datetime_demo).
-export([run/0]).

run() ->
    % Get the current OS timestamp {MegaSecs, Secs, MicroSecs}
    Timestamp = os:timestamp(),

    % Convert to local and universal time structs
    LocalTime = calendar:now_to_local_time(Timestamp),
    UtcTime = calendar:now_to_universal_time(Timestamp),

    io:format("--- Current Date and Time ---~n"),
    io:format("Local Time Struct: ~p~n", [LocalTime]),
    io:format("UTC Time Struct:   ~p~n", [UtcTime]),

    % Extract components from the local time struct
    {{Year, Month, Day}, {Hour, Min, Sec}} = LocalTime,
    io:format("~n--- Components ---~n"),
    io:format("Year: ~p, Month: ~p, Day: ~p~n", [Year, Month, Day]),
    io:format("Hour: ~p, Minute: ~p, Second: ~p~n", [Hour, Min, Sec]),

    io:format("~n--- Formatting ---~n"),
    % Erlang formatting is often done with io:format and manual composition
    io:format("Formatted (YYYY-MM-DD): ~p-~.2.0B-~.2.0B~n", [Year, Month, Day]),

    io:format("~n--- Epoch Time ---~n"),
    {MegaSecs, Secs, MicroSecs} = Timestamp,
    EpochSeconds = MegaSecs * 1000000 + Secs,
    io:format("Seconds since epoch: ~p~n", [EpochSeconds]).
maxInArray:
-module(max_in_list_demo).
-export([max_in_list/1, run/0]).

% Use a guard to ensure the list is not empty
max_in_list(List) when is_list(List), List =/= [] ->
    lists:max(List).

run() ->
    Numbers = [10, 5, 42, 17, 8, 99, 32, -5, 0],
    io:format("List: ~p~n", [Numbers]),

    MaxValue = max_in_list(Numbers),
    io:format("Maximum value: ~p~n", [MaxValue]),

    EmptyList = [],
    io:format("~nList: ~p~n", [EmptyList]),
    % This call would crash without the guard
    % io:format("Maximum value: ~p~n", [max_in_list(EmptyList)]).
    io:format("Cannot call max_in_list/1 on an empty list.~n").
